docs.title: Hi!
docs.text:
👋 Hi, I’m Ryan, a technical writer, knowledge manager, and AI enthusiast, as well as hiker, forager, baker, sci-fi nerd, and much else. This portfolio is a living, breathing documentation sandbox where I explore tools, workflows, and whatever otherwise lights me up.

🚧 This site is currently, and forever will be, under construction, so expect half-built scaffolding, scribbled notes in the margins, and lots of sparks. Oh, and definitely the odd "testing 123" placeholder or twelve. Or just as likely, bacon ipsum.

docs.title: What you'll (soon) find here:
docs.text:
• ✍️ Docs-as-Code Demos — Using MkDocs and Git to create structured, version-controlled content (that's this! Right now! How cool is that!)
  • 🤖 AI-Assisted Writing Workflows — How I use tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and others in my writing process
  • 🔄 CI/CD for Docs — Notes and experiments around automating content checks and deployments
  • 🧰 Experiments & Explorations — Just me, messing around with ideas

💬 Questions? Curiosity? Find me on LinkedIn or e-mail me to say hi.

Stay tuned!

docs.title: Project Overview: Documentation Garden Portfolio Site
docs.text: I created a technical writing portfolio project (this page!) using a docs-as-code approach. Here's what I accomplished and how:

docs.title: Technologies & Tools Used
docs.text:
• Generative AI (Claude and ChatGPT): Ideating, debugging, generating most of this summary, debugging rant receptacle
  • MkDocs with Material theme: Documentation framework for creating the site
  • Git: Version control for the project
  • GitLab: Primary repository for demonstrating Git workflow
  • GitHub Pages: Deployment platform for the live documentation site
  • Virtual Environment: For Python dependency management
  • Bash/PowerShell: Created aliases for streamlining the workflow

docs.title: Project Implementation
docs.text:
1. Repository Setup: Created repositories on both GitLab and GitHub
  2. Documentation Structure: Implemented a MkDocs site with sections for learning journey, technical guides, and style guides
  3. Deployment Process: Configured GitHub Pages to host the documentation site
  4. Workflow Automation: Created scripts/aliases to simplify pushing to multiple repositories

docs.title: Challenges Defeated
docs.text:
• Resolved Git branch conflicts between main/master
  • Fixed GitHub Pages deployment issues
  • Established connections between multiple Git remotes
  • Troubleshooted site rendering problems

docs.title: Project Outcomes
docs.text:
• Demonstrates docs-as-code principles
  • Shows Git proficiency through multi-repository management
  • Includes a live site deployed through GitHub Pages
  • Establishes a foundation for adding AI integration and CI/CD pipelines
  • Provides a platform for documenting my learning journey
